Uzbekistan MICS 2021-2022

Uzbekistan MICS 2021-2022

  • What a reader might need to know about the modeling process
    • Pipeline
    • Key takeaways for Uzbekistan
  • Loading data
  • Preprocessing individual level data
    • Dimensionality reduction
    • Imputation
  • Correlations and associations
  • Combining data for multilevel modeling
  • Modeling
    • Baseline models
    • Reduced models
  • Model comparison table
  • Model predictive performance comparison
  • Performance comparison table

Berdiyeva Sofiya

2026-03-14

What a reader might need to know about the modeling process

Pipeline

The development of models and variable selection was following the logic below:

  1. First, we run a null model to check whether multilevel approach is justified at all

  2. Next, we run a model with the most exhaustive list of predictors that are shared accross countries’ datasets, called a “model for pooling”. This model is run through anova to see whether it fits the data significantly better than the null model.

  3. After that, we do a little robustness check, running (almost) the same model for pooling through the regular logistic regression rather than multilevel one.

  4. Then, we include all variables available for the country (that are of our interest) into a “full” model and check whether it is significantly different in prediction than a model for pooling.

  5. Also, we are tracking r^2 and collinearity metrics all way along.

  6. As a next step, we remove all non-significant variables from a full model, calling this model “model_sign”, and based on it, conduct some questionable experiments with interactions and random slopes and call it ‘model_extra’

  7. Finally, we are actually doing some machine learning-style performance evaluation of all of the models, preserving the formulas, but re-training them on randomly (but within stratas) split data.

Key takeaways for Uzbekistan

  • Multilevel modeling is justified, since ICC perfectly passed 0.001 point over a commonly used threshold (ICC=0.051).

  • Robustness check shows unstable results for having more than two playthings, psychological aggression, father living in the household.

  • Significant model won in performance by accuracy equal to 0.624 (the lowest result across countries), see further metrics), and included the variables: child’s age in months [+], caregiver’s education [+], number of books in the household [+], having more than two playthings [-], car presence [+]. None of the structural predictors were significant.

  • Highest conditional R-squared reached 0.169 (See a model comparison table)

Loading data

# Indiv-level data
uzbekistan_mics <- get_and_preprocess_mics_data("Uzbekistan")

# Region-level data
region_uz <- read_csv("../data/raw/regional_data_Uzbekistan_2022.csv")
## Rows: 14 Columns: 13
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr  (2): region, region_mics
## dbl (11): popul_thsnd_total_01012022, popul_density_2022, pre_teachers_total...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Preprocessing individual level data

Dimensionality reduction

uzbekistan_mics <- uzbekistan_mics %>% 
  
  # Learning materials
  recode_playthings(books_var = "EC1",
                    playthings_vars = c("EC2A", "EC2B", "EC2C")) %>% 
  
  # Inadequate supervision
  recode_supervision(supervision_vars = c("EC3A", "EC3B")) %>% 
  
  # Child discipline - psychological aggression
  recode_psy_aggression(var1 = "UCD2D", var2 = "UCD2H") %>% 
  
  # Early stimulation and responsive care
  recode_early_stimulation_v2(
    activity_prefixes = c("EC5A", "EC5B", "EC5C", "EC5D", "EC5E", "EC5F")) %>% 
  
  # ECDI
  recode_ecdi_and_track(age_col = "CAGE",
                        ec_regular = paste0("EC", 21:38),
                        ec_frequency = "EC39",
                        ec_comparison = "EC40") %>% 
  mutate(UB8=if_else(UB8==1, 1,0)) %>% 
  mutate(HC10E=if_else(HC10E==1, 1,0)) %>% 

  
  # Social transfers
  recode_economic_assistance(unit_base = "ST4U$",
                                          number_base = "ST4N$",
                                          n_programs = 5,
                                          months_threshold = 3) %>% 
  # Caregiver's education
  recode_education_scheme("ED5A", "UZ") %>% 
  
  # Exclude children under 3
  filter(CAGE>35) %>% 
  
  # Sample one child from household
  sample_one_per_group(group_var = "house_index",
                       seed = 42) %>% 

  # Omit rows with missing values for key predictors
  filter_at(vars(ED5A, HL13, HL17), all_vars(!is.na(.)))

Imputation

uzbekistan_mics$UB8[is.na(uzbekistan_mics$UB8)] <- 0

Correlations and associations

results <- pairwise_chi_cramers_parallel(
  uzbekistan_mics[, names(uzbekistan_mics) %in% c("UB2", "ED5A","UB8","HH48", "HH51","HC10E", "HH7", "HH6", "HL13", "HL17", "windex5", "ED4", "playthings_sum", "two_plus_playthings", "supervised_inadequately", "psy_aggression", "early_stimulation", "ecdi_sum", "dev_on_track", "received_econ_assist" )], n_cores = 4, p_threshold = 0.05, use_bonferroni = FALSE)
## Loading required package: parallel
## Processing 190 pairs using 4 cores...
## Found 63 significant pairs
# Get summary
summary_stats(results)
## 
## === Association Analysis Summary ===
## 
## Total variables: 20
## Possible pairs: 190
## Computed pairs: 171
## Significant pairs: 63 (36.8%)
## Adjusted p-value threshold: 5.00e-02
## 
## --- Cramer's V Statistics (Significant Associations) ---
## Mean: 0.231
## Median: 0.169
## Min: 0.078
## Max: 1.000
## 
## --- Effect Size Distribution ---
## Weak (< 0.1): 14 (11.1%)
## Small (0.1-0.3): 88 (69.8%)
## Medium (0.3-0.5): 16 (12.7%)
## Large (>= 0.5): 8 (6.3%)
# Create heatmap
plot <- plot_cramers_heatmap(results, text_size_x = 11, text_size_y = 11)
print(plot)

# View top associations
top_20 <- get_top_associations(results, n = 20)
print(top_20)
##                  Var1                Var2  CramersV  ChiSquare        PValue
## 91     playthings_sum two_plus_playthings 1.0000000  725.00000 7.960808e-157
## 58                UB8                 ED4 0.8257593  494.36191 4.473778e-108
## 171          ecdi_sum        dev_on_track 0.7607291  419.56380  3.708228e-77
## 10               HH48                HH51 0.6883223 2060.97615  0.000000e+00
## 53                HH6             windex5 0.4346108  136.94277  1.273681e-28
## 137               UB2            ecdi_sum 0.4081760  120.79054  7.891198e-17
## 85                HH7 two_plus_playthings 0.3890378  109.72906  2.110865e-17
## 52                HH7             windex5 0.3811659  421.33349  2.900017e-59
## 28                HH7                 HH6 0.3761772  102.59423  5.204841e-16
## 148               ED4            ecdi_sum 0.3635911  191.68781  2.120463e-22
## 40               HH48                HL17 0.3118081   70.48760  1.777373e-08
## 51              HC10E             windex5 0.3109276   70.09010  2.172567e-14
## 14               HH48               HC10E 0.2834342   58.24284  2.042937e-06
## 73                HH7      playthings_sum 0.2724557  161.45487  7.737487e-17
## 127               HH7   early_stimulation 0.2667177   51.57530  1.596338e-06
## 153 early_stimulation            ecdi_sum 0.2544981   46.95775  3.621741e-04
## 112               HH7      psy_aggression 0.2516224   45.90253  1.478266e-05
## 139               UB8            ecdi_sum 0.2463360   43.99404  9.461678e-04
## 45               HL13                HL17 0.2424814   42.62800  6.620503e-11
## 43                HH7                HL17 0.2410743   42.13470  6.225326e-05

Combining data for multilevel modeling

# If we decide to grand-center anything
region_uz <- region_uz %>%
  # Log-transform to compress the scale
  mutate(popul_mln_total_01012022 = popul_thsnd_total_01012022/1000)
region_uz <- grand_mean_centering(region_uz, colnames(region_uz)[-c(1:2)])
region_uz <- region_uz %>%
  # Log-transform to compress the scale
  mutate(popul_density_2022_log = log(popul_density_2022),
         total_pre_2022_log = log(total_pre_2022),
         places_in_pre_total_2022_log = log(places_in_pre_total_2022),
         pre_teachers_total_2022_log = log(pre_teachers_total_2022))

uzb_df <- uzbekistan_mics %>%
  mutate(region_mics = as.character(as_factor(HH7))) %>% 
  left_join(region_uz,
            by = "region_mics",
            keep = FALSE)

Modeling

Baseline models

Null model

# RANDOM INTERCEPT WITH FIXED SLOPES

# Null model (unconditional model) - to calculate ICC
model_null <- glmer(
  UB8 ~ 1 + (1 | region_mics),
  data = uzb_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 100000))
)

# Calculate Intraclass Correlation Coefficient (ICC)
# ICC tells us how much variance is due to clustering
# An ICC > 0.05 or 0.10 or typically justifies using a multilevel model
vpc_null <- performance::icc(model_null)
print(paste("ICC:", round(vpc_null$ICC_adjusted, 3)))
## [1] "ICC: 0.051"

All individual predictors + universal regional predictors

# Baseline random intercept model
model_for_pooling <- glmer(
  UB8 ~ 
    # Level 1 predictors (child/household)
    CAGE + # Age in months
    ED5A + # mother's/caregiver's education
    EC1 + #Three or more books
    two_plus_playthings +
    supervised_inadequately +
    psy_aggression +
    early_stimulation +
    dev_on_track +
    HH6 + # Rural/urban
    HH48 + # household size
    HL13 + # Natural mother lives in the household
    HL17 + # Natural father lives in the household
    windex5 +
    HC10E + # Family has a car

    
    # Level 2 predictors (structure)
    popul_mln_total_01012022 +
    popul_density_2022_log +
    total_pre_2022_log +

    # Random intercept for region
    (1 | region),
  
  data = uzb_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 100000))
)

# Model summary
summary(model_for_pooling, correlation =TRUE)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula: 
## UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + two_plus_playthings + supervised_inadequately +  
##     psy_aggression + early_stimulation + dev_on_track + HH6 +  
##     HH48 + HL13 + HL17 + windex5 + HC10E + popul_mln_total_01012022 +  
##     popul_density_2022_log + total_pre_2022_log + (1 | region)
##    Data: uzb_df
## Control: glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 1e+05))
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##     945.7    1032.8    -453.8     907.7       706 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.0356 -0.8299 -0.4400  0.8863  2.6019 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  region (Intercept) 0.06365  0.2523  
## Number of obs: 725, groups:  region, 14
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                           Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)   
## (Intercept)              -8.296934   3.689542  -2.249  0.02453 * 
## CAGE                      0.030286   0.012206   2.481  0.01309 * 
## ED5A                      0.411527   0.139230   2.956  0.00312 **

## supervised_inadequately   0.314195   0.254530   1.234  0.21705

## early_stimulation         0.095061   0.219195   0.434  0.66452

## ---

## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) CAGE   ED5A   EC1    tw_pl_ sprvs_ psy_gg erly_s dv_n_t
## CAGE        -0.147                                                        
## ED5A        -0.031  0.076                                                 
## EC1         -0.011 -0.120 -0.109                                          
## tw_pls_plyt -0.003  0.074  0.037 -0.062

## windex5      0.082  0.031 -0.161 -0.119 -0.105  0.010  0.017 -0.070  0.035

## p___0101202  0.839  0.025  0.042 -0.014  0.031  0.027 -0.083 -0.004 -0.051

##             HH6    HH48   HL13   HL17   windx5 HC10E  p___01 p__202
## CAGE                                                               
## ED5A                                                               
## EC1                                                                
## tw_pls_plyt                                                        
## sprvsd_ndqt                                                        
## psy_aggrssn                                                        
## erly_stmltn                                                        
## dev_on_trck                                                        
## HH6                                                                
## HH48         0.011                                                 
## HL13         0.014  0.029                                          
## HL17        -0.008  0.164 -0.230                                   
## windex5      0.289  0.036 -0.041  0.026                            
## HC10E       -0.135 -0.187 -0.032  0.162 -0.263                     
## p___0101202  0.021 -0.004  0.013 -0.009  0.208 -0.067              
## ppl_d_2022_  0.123  0.058  0.040  0.001 -0.185  0.000 -0.252       
## ttl_p_2022_ -0.039 -0.004 -0.009 -0.008 -0.141  0.043 -0.899  0.018
## optimizer (bobyqa) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## Model is nearly unidentifiable: large eigenvalue ratio
##  - Rescale variables?
or_model <- model_for_pooling
ame_model <- avg_slopes(model_for_pooling, type = "response")

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Odds Ratios" = or_model,
    "Marginal Effects" = ame_model
  ),
  exponentiate = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  statistic = "conf.int",
  stars = TRUE,
  output = "gt"
)

+ p < 0.1, * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001 
anova(model_null, model_for_pooling)
## Data: uzb_df
## Models:
## model_null: UB8 ~ 1 + (1 | region_mics)
## model_for_pooling: UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + two_plus_playthings + supervised_inadequately + psy_aggression + early_stimulation + dev_on_track + HH6 + HH48 + HL13 + HL17 + windex5 + HC10E + popul_mln_total_01012022 + popul_density_2022_log + total_pre_2022_log + (1 | region)
##                   npar    AIC    BIC  logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## model_null           2 993.29 1002.5 -494.64    989.29                         
## model_for_pooling   19 945.65 1032.8 -453.83    907.65 81.636 17  1.962e-10 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
check_collinearity(model_for_pooling)
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##                     Term  VIF   VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##                     CAGE 1.04 [1.01, 1.27]     1.02      0.96     [0.79, 0.99]
##                     ED5A 1.10 [1.04, 1.23]     1.05      0.91     [0.81, 0.96]
##                      EC1 1.20 [1.12, 1.33]     1.10      0.83     [0.75, 0.89]
##      two_plus_playthings 1.07 [1.02, 1.22]     1.03      0.94     [0.82, 0.98]
##  supervised_inadequately 1.06 [1.01, 1.23]     1.03      0.95     [0.82, 0.99]
##           psy_aggression 1.05 [1.01, 1.23]     1.03      0.95     [0.81, 0.99]
##        early_stimulation 1.12 [1.06, 1.25]     1.06      0.89     [0.80, 0.94]
##             dev_on_track 1.06 [1.02, 1.23]     1.03      0.94     [0.82, 0.98]
##                      HH6 1.16 [1.09, 1.29]     1.08      0.86     [0.78, 0.92]
##                     HH48 1.11 [1.05, 1.24]     1.05      0.90     [0.81, 0.95]
##                     HL13 1.08 [1.03, 1.22]     1.04      0.93     [0.82, 0.97]
##                     HL17 1.15 [1.08, 1.28]     1.07      0.87     [0.78, 0.92]
##                  windex5 1.39 [1.28, 1.54]     1.18      0.72     [0.65, 0.78]
##                    HC10E 1.19 [1.12, 1.32]     1.09      0.84     [0.76, 0.90]
##   popul_density_2022_log 1.53 [1.40, 1.69]     1.24      0.66     [0.59, 0.71]
## 
## Moderate Correlation
## 
##                      Term  VIF   VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##  popul_mln_total_01012022 7.50 [6.59, 8.57]     2.74      0.13     [0.12, 0.15]
##        total_pre_2022_log 6.98 [6.13, 7.97]     2.64      0.14     [0.13, 0.16]
r2(model_for_pooling)
## # R2 for Mixed Models
## 
##   Conditional R2: 0.169
##      Marginal R2: 0.153
r2(model_null)
## # R2 for Mixed Models
## 
##   Conditional R2: 0.051
##      Marginal R2: 0.000
# Robustness check
model_fe_region <- glm(
  UB8 ~ CAGE + # Age in months
    ED5A + # mother's/caregiver's education
    EC1 + #Three or more books
    two_plus_playthings +
    supervised_inadequately +
    psy_aggression +
    early_stimulation +
    dev_on_track +
    HH6 + # Rural/urban
    HH48 + # household size
    HL13 + # Natural mother lives in the household
    HL17 + # Natural father lives in the household
    windex5 +
    HC10E + # Family has a car
    
    # Level 2 predictors (structure)
    popul_mln_total_01012022 +
    popul_density_2022_log +
    total_pre_2022_log +
    factor(region),
  data = uzb_df,
  family = binomial
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Multilevel model" = model_for_pooling,
    "Region Fixed Effects Model" = model_fe_region
  ),
  exponentiate = TRUE
)
## Model matrix is rank deficient. Parameters `factor(region)Syrdarya
##   region, factor(region)Tashkent city, factor(region)Tashkent region` were
##   not estimable.

All available country-specific variables, fixed slopes

model_full <- glmer(
    UB8 ~ 
    # Level 1 predictors (child/household)
    CAGE + # Age in months
    ED5A + # mother's/caregiver's education
    EC1 + #Three or more books
    two_plus_playthings +
    supervised_inadequately +
    psy_aggression +
    early_stimulation +
    dev_on_track +
    received_econ_assist + # Social transfers!
    HH6 + # Rural/urban
    HH48 + # household size
    HL13 + # Natural mother lives in the household
    HL17 + # Natural father lives in the household
    windex5 +
    HC10E + # Family has a car
    
    # Level 2 predictors (structure)
    popul_mln_total_01012022 +
    popul_density_2022_log +
    total_pre_2022_log +   
    places_in_pre_total_2022_log +
    pre_teachers_total_2022_log +
    labor_force_part_men_2022_c + 
    labor_force_part_women_2022_c +
    private_pre_share_c +
    
    # Random intercept for region
    (1 | region),
  
  data = uzb_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 100000))
)
## fixed-effect model matrix is rank deficient so dropping 1 column / coefficient
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
summary(model_full, correlation = TRUE)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula: 
## UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + two_plus_playthings + supervised_inadequately +  
##     psy_aggression + early_stimulation + dev_on_track + received_econ_assist +  
##     HH6 + HH48 + HL13 + HL17 + windex5 + HC10E + popul_mln_total_01012022 +  
##     popul_density_2022_log + total_pre_2022_log + places_in_pre_total_2022_log +  
##     pre_teachers_total_2022_log + labor_force_part_men_2022_c +  
##     labor_force_part_women_2022_c + private_pre_share_c + (1 |      region)

## fit warnings:
## fixed-effect model matrix is rank deficient so dropping 1 column / coefficient
## optimizer (bobyqa) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
or_model_full <- model_full
ame_model_full <- avg_slopes(model_full, type = "response")
modelsummary(
  list("Odds Ratios" = or_model_full, "Marginal Effects" = ame_model_full),
  exponentiate = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  statistic = "conf.int",
  stars = TRUE,
  output = "gt"
)

anova(model_for_pooling, model_full)
## Data: uzb_df
## Models:
## model_for_pooling: UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + two_plus_playthings + supervised_inadequately + psy_aggression + early_stimulation + dev_on_track + HH6 + HH48 + HL13 + HL17 + windex5 + HC10E + popul_mln_total_01012022 + popul_density_2022_log + total_pre_2022_log + (1 | region)
## model_full: UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + two_plus_playthings + supervised_inadequately + psy_aggression + early_stimulation + dev_on_track + received_econ_assist + HH6 + HH48 + HL13 + HL17 + windex5 + HC10E + popul_mln_total_01012022 + popul_density_2022_log + total_pre_2022_log + places_in_pre_total_2022_log + pre_teachers_total_2022_log + labor_force_part_men_2022_c + labor_force_part_women_2022_c + private_pre_share_c + (1 | region)
##                   npar    AIC    BIC  logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)   
## model_for_pooling   19 945.65 1032.8 -453.83    907.65                        
## model_full          24 936.58 1046.7 -444.29    888.58 19.066  5   0.001868 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
check_collinearity(model_full)

## 
## Low Correlation

##                     Term  VIF     VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance

r2(model_full)
## Random effect variances not available. Returned R2 does not account for random effects.
## # R2 for Mixed Models
## 
##   Conditional R2: NA
##      Marginal R2: 0.202

Reduced models

Only significant fixed effects (p < .05) from model_full

model_sign <- glmer(
    UB8 ~ 
    # Level 1 predictors (child/household)
    CAGE + # Age in months
    ED5A + # mother's/caregiver's education
    EC1 + #Three or more books
    two_plus_playthings +
    HC10E + # Family has a car
    
    # Random intercept for region
    (1 | region),  
  data = uzb_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 100000))
)

summary(model_sign, correlation = TRUE)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula: UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + two_plus_playthings + HC10E + (1 |  
##     region)
##    Data: uzb_df
## Control: glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 1e+05))
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##     938.0     970.1    -462.0     924.0       718 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.8785 -0.8391 -0.4703  0.8892  2.7541 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  region (Intercept) 0.1177   0.3431  
## Number of obs: 725, groups:  region, 14
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                     Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)         -2.82225    0.65046  -4.339 1.43e-05 ***
## CAGE                 0.02997    0.01194   2.511 0.012041 *  
## ED5A                 0.47935    0.13497   3.551 0.000383 ***
## EC1                  0.65267    0.16684   3.912 9.15e-05 ***
## two_plus_playthings -0.35908    0.17520  -2.050 0.040406 *  
## HC10E                0.51410    0.16117   3.190 0.001423 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) CAGE   ED5A   EC1    tw_pl_
## CAGE        -0.886                            
## ED5A        -0.406  0.070                     
## EC1          0.027 -0.114 -0.165              
## tw_pls_plyt -0.218  0.053  0.013 -0.049       
## HC10E       -0.060 -0.010 -0.103 -0.027 -0.038
or_model_sign <- model_sign
ame_model_sign <- avg_slopes(model_sign, type = "response")
modelsummary(
  list("Odds Ratios" = or_model_sign, "Marginal Effects" = ame_model_sign),
  exponentiate = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  statistic = "conf.int",
  stars = TRUE,
  output = "gt"
)

## Data: uzb_df
## Models:
## model_sign: UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + two_plus_playthings + HC10E + (1 | region)
## model_full: UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + two_plus_playthings + supervised_inadequately + psy_aggression + early_stimulation + dev_on_track + received_econ_assist + HH6 + HH48 + HL13 + HL17 + windex5 + HC10E + popul_mln_total_01012022 + popul_density_2022_log + total_pre_2022_log + places_in_pre_total_2022_log + pre_teachers_total_2022_log + labor_force_part_men_2022_c + labor_force_part_women_2022_c + private_pre_share_c + (1 | region)
##            npar    AIC     BIC  logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)   
## model_sign    7 937.97  970.07 -461.99    923.97                        
## model_full   24 936.58 1046.65 -444.29    888.58 35.386 17   0.005534 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
check_collinearity(model_sign)
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##                 Term  VIF     VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##                 CAGE 1.02 [1.00,   2.08]     1.01      0.98     [0.48, 1.00]
##                 ED5A 1.04 [1.01,   1.26]     1.02      0.96     [0.79, 0.99]
##                  EC1 1.04 [1.01,   1.26]     1.02      0.96     [0.79, 0.99]
##  two_plus_playthings 1.01 [1.00, 784.11]     1.00      0.99     [0.00, 1.00]
##                HC10E 1.01 [1.00,   3.47]     1.01      0.99     [0.29, 1.00]
r2(model_sign)
## # R2 for Mixed Models
## 
##   Conditional R2: 0.145
##      Marginal R2: 0.114

Extra model: interactions + random slopes

model_extra <- glmer(
  UB8 ~ 
    # Level 1 predictors (child/household)
    CAGE + # Age in months
    ED5A + # mother's/caregiver's education
    EC1 + #Three or more books
    windex5 +
    two_plus_playthings+
    HC10E + # Family has a car
    
    # Level 2 predictors (structure)
    popul_density_2022_log +
    total_pre_2022_log +
    places_in_pre_total_2022_log +
    
    # Random slopes
    (1 + windex5 | region_mics),
  
  data = uzb_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 100000))
)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
summary(model_extra, correlation = TRUE)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula: UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + windex5 + two_plus_playthings + HC10E +  
##     popul_density_2022_log + total_pre_2022_log + places_in_pre_total_2022_log +  
##     (1 + windex5 | region_mics)
##    Data: uzb_df
## Control: glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 1e+05))
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##     931.8     991.4    -452.9     905.8       712 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.0493 -0.8049 -0.4155  0.8606  2.8298 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups      Name        Variance Std.Dev. Corr 
##  region_mics (Intercept) 0.46291  0.6804        
##              windex5     0.03504  0.1872   -1.00
## Number of obs: 725, groups:  region_mics, 14
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                  -16.24596    4.52488  -3.590 0.000330 ***
## CAGE                           0.03143    0.01198   2.623 0.008705 ** 
## ED5A                           0.43548    0.13708   3.177 0.001489 ** 
## EC1                            0.63893    0.16983   3.762 0.000169 ***
## windex5                        0.11142    0.08327   1.338 0.180905    
## two_plus_playthings           -0.40114    0.17515  -2.290 0.022007 *  
## HC10E                          0.44623    0.16672   2.677 0.007438 ** 
## popul_density_2022_log        -0.24221    0.08561  -2.829 0.004665 ** 
## total_pre_2022_log            -2.10841    0.92206  -2.287 0.022217 *  
## places_in_pre_total_2022_log   2.43936    0.89222   2.734 0.006256 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) CAGE   ED5A   EC1    windx5 tw_pl_ HC10E  p__202 t__202
## CAGE        -0.179                                                        
## ED5A        -0.035  0.063                                                 
## EC1         -0.060 -0.107 -0.127                                          
## windex5      0.070  0.005 -0.147 -0.121                                   
## tw_pls_plyt -0.049  0.032  0.015 -0.028 -0.086                            
## HC10E       -0.035 -0.016 -0.058  0.014 -0.182 -0.030                     
## ppl_d_2022_  0.677 -0.042  0.021 -0.114 -0.035  0.084 -0.039              
## ttl_p_2022_  0.833 -0.059  0.002 -0.038  0.175 -0.050 -0.061  0.527       
## pl____2022_ -0.941  0.059 -0.009  0.057 -0.150  0.036  0.052 -0.643 -0.966
## optimizer (bobyqa) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
or_model_extra <- model_extra
ame_model_extra <- avg_slopes(model_extra, type = "response")
modelsummary(
  list("Odds Ratios" = or_model_extra, "Marginal Effects" = ame_model_extra),
  exponentiate = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  statistic = "conf.int",
  stars = TRUE,
  output = "gt"
)
Odds Ratios Marginal Effects
(Intercept) 0.000***
[0.000, 0.001]
CAGE 1.032** 0.007**
[1.008, 1.056] [0.002, 0.012]

[1.182, 2.022] [0.038, 0.151]

[1.358, 2.643] [0.068, 0.216]

[0.950, 1.316] [-0.012, 0.057]

[0.475, 0.944] [-0.162, -0.013]

[1.127, 2.166] [0.026, 0.171]

[0.664, 0.928] [-0.088, -0.017]

+ p < 0.1, * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001 
anova(model_sign, model_extra)
## Data: uzb_df
## Models:
## model_sign: UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + two_plus_playthings + HC10E + (1 | region)
## model_extra: UB8 ~ CAGE + ED5A + EC1 + windex5 + two_plus_playthings + HC10E + popul_density_2022_log + total_pre_2022_log + places_in_pre_total_2022_log + (1 + windex5 | region_mics)
##             npar    AIC    BIC  logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)   
## model_sign     7 937.97 970.07 -461.99    923.97                        
## model_extra   13 931.82 991.44 -452.91    905.82 18.151  6   0.005864 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
check_collinearity(model_extra)
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##                    Term  VIF     VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##                    CAGE 1.02 [ 1.00,  1.83]     1.01      0.98     [0.55, 1.00]
##                    ED5A 1.06 [ 1.01,  1.23]     1.03      0.95     [0.81, 0.99]
##                     EC1 1.07 [ 1.02,  1.22]     1.03      0.94     [0.82, 0.98]
##                 windex5 1.16 [ 1.08,  1.28]     1.07      0.87     [0.78, 0.92]
##     two_plus_playthings 1.03 [ 1.00,  1.41]     1.01      0.97     [0.71, 1.00]
##                   HC10E 1.05 [ 1.01,  1.24]     1.02      0.95     [0.81, 0.99]
##  popul_density_2022_log 2.33 [ 2.09,  2.62]     1.53      0.43     [0.38, 0.48]
## 
## High Correlation
## 
##                          Term   VIF     VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance
##            total_pre_2022_log 19.89 [17.31, 22.88]     4.46      0.05
##  places_in_pre_total_2022_log 24.51 [21.32, 28.21]     4.95      0.04
##  Tolerance 95% CI
##      [0.04, 0.06]
##      [0.04, 0.05]
r2(model_extra)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## Random effect variances not available. Returned R2 does not account for random effects.
## # R2 for Mixed Models
## 
##   Conditional R2: NA
##      Marginal R2: 0.153

Model comparison table

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Null"         = model_null,
    "For Pooling"  = model_for_pooling,
    "Full"         = model_full,
    "Significant"  = model_sign,
    "Extra"        = model_extra
  ),
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  statistic = "conf.int",
  stars = TRUE,
  gof_map = c("nobs", "logLik", "AIC", "BIC", "icc", "r2.marginal", "r2.conditional"),
  output = "gt"
)

SD (Intercept region_mics) 0.419 0.680

R2 Cond. 0.051 0.169 0.145
+ p < 0.1, * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001 
# Plots
# Coefficient plot (OR forest plot) for model_sign

coef_df <- tidy(model_sign, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    sig = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "p < .001",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "p < .01",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "p < .05",
      TRUE            ~ "n.s."
    ),
    sig = factor(sig, levels = c("p < .001", "p < .01", "p < .05", "n.s."))
  )

p_coef <- ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = estimate, y = reorder(term, estimate), color = sig)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("p < .001" = "#d73027", "p < .01" = "#f46d43",
               "p < .05" = "#fdae61", "n.s." = "grey60")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Odds Ratios — Significant Model",
    x        = "Odds Ratio (95% CI)",
    y        = NULL,
    color    = "Significance"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_coef)
## `height` was translated to `width`.

# Marginal effects plot (AME) 
ame_df <- as.data.frame(ame_model_sign) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    sig = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "p < .001",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "p < .01",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "p < .05",
      TRUE            ~ "n.s."
    ),
    sig = factor(sig, levels = c("p < .001", "p < .01", "p < .05", "n.s."))
  )

p_ame <- ggplot(ame_df, aes(x = estimate, y = reorder(term, estimate), color = sig)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("p < .001" = "#d73027", "p < .01" = "#f46d43",
               "p < .05" = "#fdae61", "n.s." = "grey60")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Marginal Effects — Significant Model",
    x     = "AME on Pr(UB8=1)",
    y     = NULL,
    color = "Significance"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_ame)
## `height` was translated to `width`.

#  Predicted probabilities for key predictors
pred_ed5a <- ggpredict(model_sign, terms = "ED5A [all]", bias_correction = TRUE)
p_ed5a <- plot(pred_ed5a) +
  labs(title = "Predicted Probability by Mother's Education (ED5A)",
       x = "Education Level", y = "Pr(UB8 = 1)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

pred_windex <- ggpredict(model_sign, terms = "HC10E [all]", bias_correction = TRUE)
p_windex <- plot(pred_windex) +
  labs(title = "Predicted Probability by Car Ownership",
       x = "Car Ownership (HC10E)", y = "Pr(UB8 = 1)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_ed5a)

print(p_windex)

#  4. Random effects (regional variation) 
re_df <- ranef(model_sign, condVar = TRUE)$region %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("region") %>%
  rename(intercept = `(Intercept)`) %>%
  mutate(
    se = sqrt(attr(ranef(model_sign, condVar = TRUE)$region, "postVar")[1, 1, ]),
    lower = intercept - 1.96 * se,
    upper = intercept + 1.96 * se
  )

p_re <- ggplot(re_df, aes(x = intercept, y = reorder(region, intercept))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Regional Random Intercepts (±1.96 SE)",
    x     = "Random Intercept (log-odds scale)",
    y     = "Region"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_re)
## `height` was translated to `width`.

#  5. Random slopes by region (model_extra: windex5 | region_mics) 
re_slopes_df <- ranef(model_extra, condVar = TRUE)$region_mics %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("region") %>%
  rename(intercept = `(Intercept)`, slope_windex = windex5)

p_slopes <- ggplot(re_slopes_df, aes(x = intercept, y = slope_windex, label = region)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "steelblue") +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Random Intercepts vs. Random Slopes for Wealth Index by Region",
    x     = "Random Intercept",
    y     = "Random Slope (windex5)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_slopes)

#  ICC and R² across models — summary bar chart 
model_list <- list(
  "Null"        = model_null,
  "For Pooling" = model_for_pooling,
  "Full"        = model_full,
  "Significant" = model_sign,
  "Extra"       = model_extra
)

r2_df <- purrr::map_dfr(names(model_list), function(nm) {
  r2_vals <- r2(model_list[[nm]])
  icc_result <- tryCatch(
    performance::icc(model_list[[nm]])$ICC_adjusted,
    error = function(e) NA_real_
  )
  
  tibble::tibble(
    model        = nm,
    R2_marginal  = r2_vals$R2_marginal,
    R2_conditional = r2_vals$R2_conditional,
    ICC          = icc_result
  )
}) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-model, names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels = names(model_list)))
## Random effect variances not available. Returned R2 does not account for random effects.
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## Random effect variances not available. Returned R2 does not account for random effects.
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
p_r2 <- ggplot(r2_df, aes(x = model, y = value, fill = metric)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("R2_marginal" = "#4575b4", "R2_conditional" = "#74add1", "ICC" = "#f46d43"),
    labels = c("ICC", "R² Conditional", "R² Marginal")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Model Fit Comparison: R² and ICC",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Value",
    fill  = NULL
  ) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_r2)

Model predictive performance comparison

# Splitting data

uzb_df <- uzb_df %>%
  mutate(strata_var = paste0(region_mics, "_", UB8))

train_idx <- createDataPartition(uzb_df$strata_var, p = 0.75, list = FALSE)
train_df  <- uzb_df[train_idx, ]
test_df   <- uzb_df[-train_idx, ]


# Refit all models on training data
# Swapping uzb_df to train_df in each model call
# Example:
model_null_train <- update(model_null, data = train_df)
model_pool_train <- update(model_for_pooling, data = train_df)
model_full_train <- update(model_full, data = train_df)
## fixed-effect model matrix is rank deficient so dropping 1 column / coefficient
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
model_sign_train <- update(model_sign, data = train_df)
model_extra_train <- update(model_extra, data = train_df)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
# Predict test set
# allow.new.levels = TRUE handles regions in test not seen in train
predict_prob <- function(model, newdata) {
  predict(model, newdata = newdata, type = "response", allow.new.levels = TRUE)
}

probs <- list(
  Null = predict_prob(model_null_train, test_df),
  For_Pooling = predict_prob(model_pool_train, test_df),
  Full = predict_prob(model_full_train, test_df),
  Significant = predict_prob(model_sign_train, test_df),
  Extra = predict_prob(model_extra_train, test_df)
)


# Metrics

metrics_df <- purrr::map2_dfr(
  probs, names(probs),
  ~ compute_metrics(.x, as.numeric(as.character(test_df$UB8)), .y)
)

Performance comparison table

metrics_df %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%
  gt::gt() %>%
  gt::tab_header(title = "Out-of-Sample Prediction Accuracy Comparison") %>%
  gt::fmt_number(columns = where(is.numeric), decimals = 3) %>%
  gt::cols_label(
    Model       = "Model",
    AUC         = "AUC",
    Accuracy    = "Accuracy",
    Sensitivity = "Sensitivity",
    Specificity = "Specificity",
    PPV         = "Precision (PPV)",
    F1          = "F1 Score",
    Brier       = "Brier Score",
    LogLoss     = "Log Loss"
  ) %>%
  gt::tab_style(
    style = gt::cell_fill(color = "#d4edda"),
    locations = gt::cells_body(
      rows = Accuracy == max(Accuracy)
    )
  )
Out-of-Sample Prediction Accuracy Comparison
Model AUC Accuracy Sensitivity Specificity Precision (PPV) F1 Score Brier Score Log Loss
Null 0.612 0.565 0.234 0.839 0.545 0.327 0.237 0.666
For_Pooling 0.648 0.600 0.597 0.602 0.554 0.575 0.234 0.660
Full 0.666 0.612 0.597 0.624 0.568 0.582 0.229 0.645
Significant 0.660 0.624 0.584 0.656 0.584 0.584 0.228 0.645
Extra 0.667 0.618 0.636 0.602 0.570 0.601 0.229 0.645
 
# ROC curves plot (all models)
roc_list <- purrr::map2(probs, names(probs), function(p, nm) {
  roc_obj <- roc(as.numeric(as.character(test_df$UB8)), p, quiet = TRUE)
  data.frame(
    model       = paste0(nm, " (AUC=", round(auc(roc_obj), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_obj$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_obj$specificities
  )
})

roc_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(roc_list)

p_roc <- ggplot(roc_df, aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity, color = model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  labs(
    title = "ROC Curves — Out-of-Sample Performance",
    x     = "1 - Specificity (FPR)",
    y     = "Sensitivity (TPR)",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_roc)

# Calibration plot — predicted vs. observed probabilities
cal_df <- purrr::map2_dfr(probs, names(probs), function(p, nm) {
  data.frame(
    model    = nm,
    pred     = p,
    observed = as.numeric(as.character(test_df$UB8))
  )
})

# Bin predictions into deciles per model
cal_binned <- cal_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(model) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(bin = ntile(pred, 10)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(model, bin) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_pred = mean(pred),
    mean_obs  = mean(observed),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

p_cal <- ggplot(cal_binned, aes(x = mean_pred, y = mean_obs, color = model)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  labs(
    title = "Calibration Plot — Predicted vs. Observed Probabilities",
    x     = "Mean Predicted Probability",
    y     = "Mean Observed Proportion",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_cal)
